name: GitHub Issue to Codex Dispatch
purpose: Claim Codex-ready GitHub issues created from Plane and request Codex by commenting @codex on the GitHub issue.
eligibility: Labels include plane, codex-ready, and automation.
Labels do not include codex-in-progress, codex-pr-open, done, or blocked.
Issue body includes plane_issue_id and plane_project_id.
Issue is not a pull request.
core_logic: Parse Plane metadata from the GitHub issue body.
Claim the GitHub issue with codex-in-progress.
Comment on the issue with an @codex implementation request and required PR metadata.
Resolve the Plane Building state by name within the issue project.
Move the Plane item to Building only after the Codex request comment is created.
Ignore duplicate or already-claimed webhook deliveries.
codex_comment:
@codex please implement this issue.

Use the issue body as the source of truth. Create a feature branch, make the code changes, run appropriate validation, and open a PR against main.

Include plane_issue_id, plane_project_id, github_issue_number, and plane_url in the PR body.

Do not deploy from Codex.
safe_failure_handling: Do not dispatch if required metadata is missing.
Do not dispatch if the issue is already claimed or has an open PR label.
Use GitHub labels as visible claim state.
Use Plane project ID from issue metadata, not a hardcoded project ID.
Routine dispatch should not notify Slack.
Do not use the OpenAI API for this workflow; the Codex request travels through GitHub using the existing GitHub credential.
